quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Oxidation-Reduction (Redox) Reactions
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Redox Reactions, Oxidation Numbers, Oxidizing Agents, Reducing Agents, Identifying Oxidation and Reduction

questions.question: When $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ reacts with HCl, the products are:
questions.explanation: Sodium carbonate reacts with hydrochloric acid to produce sodium chloride, carbon dioxide, and water: $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{NaCl} + \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$. This is not a redox reaction.

questions.question: In the reaction $\text{Zn} + \text{CuSO}_4 \rightarrow \text{ZnSO}_4 + \text{Cu}$, which species is oxidized?
questions.explanation: Zinc is oxidized because its oxidation number increases from 0 in Zn to +2 in $\text{ZnSO}_4$. Oxidation is the loss of electrons.

questions.question: Which of the following reactions is not a redox reaction?
questions.explanation: $\text{NaOH} + \text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{NaCl} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$ is not a redox reaction. It is an acid-base neutralization reaction with no change in oxidation numbers. Na remains +1, O remains -2, H remains +1 (in both reactants and products), and Cl remains -1. The other reactions all involve changes in oxidation states: B (Zn 0→+2, H +1→0), C (Fe 0→+2, Cu +2→0), and D (K 0→+1, Cl 0→-1).

questions.question: In the reaction $\text{Zn} + \text{CuSO}_4 \rightarrow \text{ZnSO}_4 + \text{Cu}$, which species is reduced?
questions.explanation: Copper is reduced because its oxidation number decreases from +2 in $\text{CuSO}_4$ to 0 in Cu. Reduction is the gain of electrons.

questions.question: Which of the following is a redox reaction?
questions.explanation: $2\text{Na} + \text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NaCl}$ is a redox reaction because sodium is oxidized (0 → +1) and chlorine is reduced (0 → -1). The other reactions are acid-base, precipitation, or decomposition with no change in oxidation numbers.

questions.question: In the reaction $2\text{Fe} + 3\text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{FeCl}_3$, what is oxidized?
questions.explanation: Iron is oxidized because its oxidation number increases from 0 in Fe to +3 in $\text{FeCl}_3$.

questions.question: In the reaction $2\text{Fe} + 3\text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{FeCl}_3$, what is reduced?
questions.explanation: Chlorine is reduced because its oxidation number decreases from 0 in $\text{Cl}_2$ to -1 in $\text{FeCl}_3$.

questions.question: In the reaction $2\text{K} + \text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl}$, the oxidation number of K changes from:
questions.explanation: Potassium's oxidation number changes from 0 (elemental form) to +1 in KCl. This is oxidation.

questions.question: In the reaction $2\text{K} + \text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl}$, the oxidation number of Cl changes from:
questions.explanation: Chlorine's oxidation number changes from 0 in $\text{Cl}_2$ to -1 in KCl. This is reduction.

questions.question: In $\text{Zn} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{ZnCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$, the reducing agent is:
questions.explanation: Zinc is the reducing agent because it causes reduction (donates electrons) and is itself oxidized. Zn loses electrons (0 → +2).

questions.question: In $\text{Zn} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{ZnCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$, the oxidizing agent is:
questions.explanation: The hydrogen ion ($\text{H}^+$) is the oxidizing agent because it accepts electrons (is reduced) from zinc. $\text{H}^+$ is reduced to $\text{H}_2$ (oxidation number +1 → 0).

questions.question: In the reaction $2\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 3\text{C} \rightarrow 4\text{Fe} + 3\text{CO}_2$, what is oxidized?
questions.explanation: Carbon is oxidized because its oxidation number increases from 0 in C to +4 in $\text{CO}_2$.

questions.question: In the reaction $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$, which species is reduced?
questions.explanation: Chlorine is reduced because its oxidation number decreases from +5 in $\text{KClO}_3$ to -1 in KCl. Oxygen is oxidized (from -2 to 0 in $\text{O}_2$). This is a decomposition redox reaction.

questions.question: In $2\text{Al} + 3\text{CuCl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{AlCl}_3 + 3\text{Cu}$, the oxidizing agent is:
questions.explanation: $\text{CuCl}_2$ (specifically the $\text{Cu}^{2+}$ ion) is the oxidizing agent because it accepts electrons (is reduced) from aluminum. $\text{Cu}^{2+}$ is reduced to Cu (oxidation number +2 → 0).

questions.question: In the reaction $2\text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{O}_2$, oxygen in $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2$ is:
questions.explanation: This is a disproportionation reaction. In $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2$, oxygen has an oxidation number of -1. Some oxygen atoms are reduced to -2 in $\text{H}_2\text{O}$, and some are oxidized to 0 in $\text{O}_2$. Therefore, oxygen is both oxidized and reduced.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered redox reactions and oxidation numbers! ⚡🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the definitions of oxidizing and reducing agents.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on tracking oxidation number changes.
scoreMessages.0: Please review oxidation numbers, oxidation vs reduction, and how to identify redox reactions.
